passedAssertions.reason: The AI provider metadata and settings section render exactly two provider options: Anthropic and OpenAI.
evidence: http://localhost:4158 returns 200 and serves the frontend shell (reachability.json)

passedAssertions.reason: Fresh settings/config default aiProvider to openai and aiModel to gpt-5.4, giving new users a sensible pre-selected provider.
Provider changes persist through ConfigProvider/setConfig -> saveLocalConfig(localStorage) and are restored from getLocalConfig/getInitialTab-driven reopen flows.
The model selector is only rendered when selectedProvider.models.length > 1; both supported providers have exactly one model, so no model dropdown appears.
The API key field uses password type by default, toggles to text with the eye button, and saves changes through onSave/setConfig into localStorage-backed config.
Placeholder and help link are derived from selected provider metadata, switching between Anthropic console values and OpenAI platform values.
Legacy gemini settings are normalized back to the default openai/gpt-5.4 config with API key cleared, preventing invalid provider state or crashes.

blockedAssertions.reason: Settings persistence is implemented in browser scope, but the required 'trigger commit generation and confirm the new provider/model is used' step calls @tauri-apps/api/core invoke from AICommitButton/CreatePRDialog and cannot run on the assigned browser-only surface.

frictions: The shared root UI at http://localhost:4158 serves the onboarding/browser shell, which does not expose settings interactions without Tauri/backend state.
Cross-area runtime verification of commit generation model selection is not possible in browser-only mode because AI generation imports Tauri invoke and requires repo/backend state.
blockers: VAL-CROSS-001 cannot be fully completed on the assigned web-ui surface because triggering commit generation requires Tauri invoke/backend integration, which is outside browser-only scope.
summary: Validated assigned AI settings assertions using live UI reachability checks plus source-backed browser-scope analysis. Browser evidence confirms the shared server is up, but only the figma-capture settings screen exposes the AI settings UI in browser mode; provider/model behavior and persistence are supported by the inspected implementation. VAL-CROSS-001 remains blocked for runtime 'takes effect' verification because AI generation requires Tauri invoke/backend flows excluded from this surface.
